failed_rules.message: LoanType must be Closed End Second for 2nd Lien program
US Citizens are not eligible for Select ITIN program.
Loan amount $753,750 below minimum $4,500,001 for FICO Score 780, Primary Residence, SFR
Reserves 12 months does not meet requirement of 18 months PITIA. Note: Cash-Out proceeds may NOT be used.
Income Doc Type "Bank Stmts: 12 Mo. Personal" is not a DSCR type - Investor DSCR requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL
Occupancy 'Primary Residence' not eligible for Foreign National. Available: Investment Property, Second Home
US Citizens are not eligible for Foreign National program - FAIL
Income Doc Type "Bank Stmts: 12 Mo. Personal" is not a DSCR type - DSCR Supreme Program requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL
Income Doc Type "Bank Stmts: 12 Mo. Personal" is not a DSCR type - DSCR Multi (5-8 Units) requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL
Income Doc Type "Bank Stmts: 12 Mo. Personal" is not a DSCR type - Investor DSCR No Ratio requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL

specifications: Must be a tri-merge or Residential Mortgage Credit Report (RMCR) from approved credit reporting agencies (Experian, TransUnion, Equifax)
Must include all borrowers on the loan
Must verify Social Security Number for borrower
Must display credit scores from all three bureaus
Must show complete tradeline details including account history, balances, and payment status
Must show mortgage/housing payment history if applicable
Must include all inquiries, public records, disputes, collections, charge-offs, and alerts
Must certify results of public record searches for each city where borrower resided in last 2 years
Must be dated within program recency requirements
reasons_needed: Credit score of 780 must be verified to confirm program eligibility for Flex Select
Tradeline requirements must be validated (3 tradelines 12+ months OR 2 tradelines 24+ months with activity in last 12 months)
Credit report is required to identify any credit events, inquiries, disputes, or housing history
Public records search required to verify no undisclosed judgments, liens, or bankruptcies
Tri-merge report required per NQM Funding guidelines for all borrowers
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQM Funding Underwriting Guidelines - Credit Report section

specifications: Must be valid (not expired) government-issued photo identification
Must display borrower's full legal name matching loan application
Must include photograph, signature, and date of birth
Acceptable forms: Driver's License, Passport, State ID, Military ID

reasons_needed.reason: Identity verification is required for all borrowers before underwriting can proceed
Borrower name on ID must reconcile with all loan documents, credit report, and title

specifications: Must be fully executed and signed by borrower
Must include correct borrower name, SSN, and current address
Must specify tax years to be requested (typically last 2 years)
Must be dated within 120 days of closing
Signature must match borrower's legal name on loan application

reasons_needed.reason: Per NQMF guidelines, a fully executed and signed IRS Form-4506C is required for all income documentation
Required to authorize lender to obtain tax transcripts for income verification

specifications: Must include all pages, addenda, and amendments
Must be fully executed with all party signatures and dates
Must identify subject property address matching loan application
Must state purchase price of $1,020,000 consistent with loan scenario
Must identify buyer as Marlene A Melendez Niccum or entity taking title
Must include earnest money deposit amount and holder
Must include closing date and contingency dates

reasons_needed.reason: Purchase transaction requires executed contract to verify purchase price, parties, and terms
Contract validates LTV calculation: $753,750 loan amount / $1,020,000 purchase price = 73.9% LTV

specifications: Must cover most recent 12 months of payment history
Must identify borrower name and property address for each mortgage held
Must show detailed payment history with dates and amounts
Must indicate any late payments (30/60/90/120 day delinquencies)
Must be from current mortgage servicer or reflected on credit report
Must support 0x30x12 housing history requirement if applicable
reasons_needed: Housing history verification required per NQM Funding guidelines for all programs
12-month 0x30 housing history required to verify no late mortgage payments
Mortgage payment history may satisfy tradeline requirement (24 month mortgage history with 0x30x12)
Late payments on existing mortgages may affect qualification or pricing
Required to confirm borrower meets housing history standards for Flex Select program
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQM Funding Underwriting Guidelines - Housing History and Standard Tradelines sections
document_requests.condition: Required if borrower has existing mortgage(s) - if credit report shows complete 12-month mortgage payment history with no lates, separate verification may not be needed

specifications: Must include 12 consecutive months of statements from primary personal bank account
Must include all pages of each monthly statement
Must show account holder name matching borrower name
Must show financial institution name and account number or partial account number
Must show beginning and ending balance for each month
Must show all deposits clearly for income calculation
Must identify transfers from business account(s) if applicable
Must be dated within the most recent 12 months prior to application date
Statements must be complete with no missing months
Must provide 2 most recent consecutive months of statements
Must include all pages of each statement
Must show borrower name as account holder
Must show financial institution name and logo
Must show account number (full or masked acceptable)
Must show beginning and ending balances for each month
Must show complete transaction history
Statements must be dated within 90 days of note date per NQMF guidelines
Must show sufficient verified funds for down payment, closing costs, prepaid items, and reserves
Large deposits exceeding 50% of qualifying income require sourcing documentation
Must document sufficient liquid reserves beyond funds needed for closing
Reserves calculated as months of PITIA (Principal, Interest, Taxes, Insurance, Association dues)
Bank statements provided for funds to close may satisfy reserve verification if sufficient balance remains post-closing
Must show assets are liquid and accessible (checking, savings, money market accounts)
Assets must be dated within 90 days of note date
Gift funds may NOT be used to meet reserve requirements per NQMF guidelines
reasons_needed: Bank Statement (12 Mo. Personal) income documentation type requires 12 consecutive months of personal bank statements
Qualifying income is calculated as sum of total eligible deposits divided by 12 months
Only transfers or deposits from business account(s) are considered eligible deposits per guidelines
Account ownership and deposit pattern must be verified to support qualifying income calculation
NQMF guidelines require assets be verified for down payment, closing costs, prepaid items, debt payoff, and reserves
Assets must be from acceptable sources per NQMF guidelines
Account ownership must be confirmed to borrower
73.9% LTV purchase requires verification of approximately 26.1% equity contribution
Reserve requirements must be supported by verified liquid assets
Reserve requirements apply based on loan program, occupancy, and property type
Post-closing liquidity demonstrates borrower's ability to manage mortgage payments
NQMF requires verification of reserves from acceptable asset sources
Primary residence purchase at 73.9% LTV may have reserve requirements per program matrix
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Bank Statement Income - CALCULATING QUALIFYING INCOME

specifications: Must be prepared and signed by a CPA, EA, or PTIN licensed tax preparer
Must be dated within 120 days of loan closing
Must specify business expenses as a percentage of gross annual sales/revenue
Must cover the same 12-month period as the bank statements
The preparer must attest they have prepared or reviewed the borrower's most recent tax return
Minimum 20% expense ratio is required if using third-party expense statement option
Must include preparer's license number and contact information
reasons_needed: P&L Statement is required to calculate expense ratio for 12-month bank statement program
Third-party P&L allows for expense ratio calculation other than default 50%
Guidelines require P&L to be signed and dated by CPA/EA or PTIN licensed tax preparer
Expense ratio determines net qualifying income from gross deposits
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Bank Statement Income - OPTION 2/OPTION 3 EXPENSE STATEMENT

specifications: Must provide copy of cancelled check or wire confirmation for EMD
Must show EMD amount matches purchase contract
Must show funds sourced from borrower's verified account
Must include evidence EMD was deposited with escrow/title company
Copy of deposit receipt from escrow holder acceptable
If EMD from account not previously disclosed, must provide bank statement showing source
reasons_needed: Purchase transaction requires verification of earnest money deposit
EMD must be sourced to confirm acceptable origin of funds
EMD contributes to borrower's funds to close calculation
Title company must confirm receipt of deposit for settlement
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Guidelines - ASSETS section (funds to close verification)

specifications: Must be completed on FNMA Form 1004 (Uniform Residential Appraisal Report) for 1-unit SFR property
Must identify subject property address and confirm property type as single-family residence
Must include appraised value to support LTV/CLTV calculation at 73.9%
Must be dated within 120 days of Note date; if exceeds 120 days, recertification will be required (max 365 days from Note date)
Must include all pages, addenda, and appraiser certification
Must include street map showing location of subject property and all comparables used
Must include exterior building sketch with dimensions and floor plan sketch with gross living area calculations
Must include original color photographs: front view, street view, rear view, kitchen, all bathrooms, main living area
Must include comparable sales analysis with sales comparison approach completed
Must confirm property is in average or better condition (C4 or better rating)
Must disclose any repairs, adverse conditions, deferred maintenance, or health/safety issues
Must confirm property meets minimum property standards: year-round use, full kitchen/bathroom, no environmental hazards
Must include appraiser's license/certification and Errors & Omissions insurance confirmation
Must reconcile borrower/owner information with loan file
reasons_needed: Collateral valuation is required for underwriting the $753,750 purchase loan
LTV/CLTV eligibility at 73.9% depends on verified appraised value
Property type and condition affect program eligibility under Flex Select guidelines
NQMF guidelines require Form 1004 for 1-unit residential properties
Subject property identity must be consistent across the loan file
Appraisal age requirements must be met per NQMF policy (120 days for currency, 365 days maximum)

specifications: Must include Submission Summary Report (SSR) from UCDP portal
Must show SSR Score - score of 2.5 or less required for appraisal acceptance per NQMF guidelines
Must identify subject property address matching appraisal report
Must confirm appraisal was submitted to UCDP portal
Must disclose any flags, warnings, or overvaluation indicators
reasons_needed: NQMF Appraisal Review Process requires secondary appraisal product to support valuation
SSR Score of 2.5 or less is primary acceptable review product per guidelines
Validates appraisal data integrity and compliance with industry standards
Required to identify potential collateral risk issues before underwriting completion

specifications: Must be a FEMA-standard Life of Loan Flood Determination Certificate
Must identify subject property address accurately
Must indicate flood zone designation (Section B4)
Must indicate whether property is in Special Flood Hazard Area (SFHA) - Zone A or Zone V
Must indicate NFIP community participation status if in SFHA
Must indicate whether improvements are in the flood zone
Must be issued by certified flood determination provider
reasons_needed: Required to determine whether flood insurance is required for the subject property
Properties in SFHA (Zone A or V) require flood insurance per NQMF and federal requirements
Properties in Zone A or V must be in FEMA-participating community to be eligible for financing
Flood zone must be reconciled with appraisal report flood zone notation

specifications: Must be hazard insurance policy protecting against fire and standard extended coverage perils
Must include coverage for: fire/lightning, explosion, windstorm (including named storms), hail, smoke, aircraft, vehicles, riot/civil commotion
Must provide replacement cost coverage (actual cash value policies not acceptable)
Must name all borrowers as insureds matching names on the note (Marlene A Melendez Niccum)
Must list property address matching the note/security instrument
Must include standard mortgagee clause naming NQM Funding, LLC (or assigns) as loss payee
Must be effective for at least 60 days after funding date
Must show policy number, insurance company name, agent information
Must show effective and expiration dates of coverage
Must show premium amount and deductible
Must include coverage amount sufficient to cover replacement cost of improvements
Policy must require insurer to notify mortgagee before cancellation
reasons_needed: Hazard insurance is required to protect collateral for the mortgage loan
NQMF guidelines require replacement cost coverage with specified perils
Mortgagee clause protects lender interest in the collateral
Evidence of insurance must be verified prior to closing
California property requires standard hazard coverage per guidelines

specifications: Must identify all borrowers and vested parties on the subject property
Must include subject property address and full legal description
Must disclose all liens, judgments, easements, encumbrances, and title exceptions
Must show title insurer name and commitment effective date
Must be dated within 90 days of closing or updated prior to closing
Must include 24-month chain of title history per NQM guidelines
Must confirm proposed lien position as first lien without exception
Must identify any requirements or conditions to be satisfied prior to policy issuance
reasons_needed: Title must confirm ownership and insurable interest for the subject property
Chain of title must be reviewed for inconsistencies or flipping activity per NQM guidelines
All liens, judgments, and exceptions must be identified to ensure first lien position
Title exceptions must be reviewed against allowable exceptions per NQM guidelines
Vesting must reconcile with borrower identity and transaction structure
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQM Guidelines - Title Commitment Review: All files are to contain a 24-month title history; vesting history should be reviewed for inconsistencies or any indication of flipping activity

specifications: Must be the recorded grant deed or warranty deed conveying title to the property
Must identify the grantee(s) matching the borrower name(s) on the loan application
Must include the full legal description of the subject property
Must show recording information (date, instrument number, county recorder)
Must reflect current ownership and vesting type (sole owner, joint tenants, etc.)
For purchase transaction: seller's vesting deed establishing seller's ownership required
reasons_needed: Deed confirms legal ownership of the property being purchased
Vesting must match borrower identity per loan application
Legal description must match title commitment and appraisal
For purchase: seller's ownership must be verified to convey clear title to buyer
Final title policy vesting should reflect the name(s) of the individual borrower(s) per NQM guidelines
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQM Guidelines - Vesting: Final title policy vesting should reflect the name(s) of the individual borrower(s)

specifications: Must certify borrower's intent to occupy property as primary residence
Must include borrower signature and date
Must reference subject property address
Must acknowledge occupancy within 60 days of closing and continuous occupancy for at least one year

reasons_needed.reason: Primary residence occupancy declared in loan scenario - certification required to confirm intent
Occupancy type determines LTV limits, reserve requirements, and program eligibility

specifications: Must be on CPA or tax preparer letterhead
Must confirm borrower's self-employment status and business ownership
Must confirm length of time in business (minimum 2 years typically required)
Must be signed and dated by the CPA or licensed tax preparer
Must include preparer's license credentials and contact information
reasons_needed: Self-employment history and business continuity must be verified
CPA letter supports legitimacy of business for bank statement income program
Business ownership percentage determines usable income calculation

specifications: Required only if bank statements contain large or unusual deposits
Must identify source of each large deposit
Must explain if deposit is business income, transfer, gift, loan, or other
Must be signed and dated by borrower
Supporting documentation for deposit source may be required
reasons_needed: Large or unusual deposits may require sourcing per bank statement program guidelines
Transfers, refunds, loans, and non-business deposits must be excluded from income calculation
Only eligible deposits from business activity can be used to calculate qualifying income
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Bank Statement Income - Deposit Analysis
